rationale: The cited paper appears potentially foundational: it provides evidence that incidental environmental cues and processing fluency affect product evaluation and choice, which strongly overlaps the citing paper’s mechanism-focused account (spatial cues → safety/risk accessibility → processing fluency → risky choice). The citing note explicitly says it “extends prior research on store atmospherics and environmental cue effects,” making this citation plausibly substantive rather than incidental. With a confirmed DOI-level match (10.1509/jmkr.45.1.001), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
